# REST API Endpoint

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED:START - Do not edit manually -->

The module exposes **one REST endpoint** — a webhook receiver for async label generation callbacks from DPD.

## `POST /V1/dpd-shipping/callback`

**File:** `etc/webapi.xml`, implemented by `Model/ApiCallback.php`

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Method | `POST` |
| URL | `/rest/{store_code}/V1/dpd-shipping/callback` |
| Authentication | **Anonymous** — no Magento token required |
| Interface | `DpdConnect\Shipping\Api\ApiCallbackInterface::sendCallback()` |

> **Security note:** This endpoint has `<resource ref="anonymous"/>`, meaning any HTTP client can call it. DPD calls it after async label generation completes. No additional Magento auth is enforced; the integrity check relies on the fact that the `jobId` must match an existing batch job in the database.

### When it is called

DPD calls this URL for each async job after the shipment is processed on their side. The URL is set in the async create request as `callbackURI`.

The default URL generated by the module is:
```
https://your-store.com/rest/default/V1/dpd-shipping/callback
```

### Request Body (sent by DPD)

```json
{
  "jobid": "abc-123-def",
  "shipment": {
    "orderId": "000000123",
    "product": {
      "productCode": "B2C"
    },
    "trackingInfo": {
      "parcelNumbers": ["01234567890"],
      "shipmentIdentifier": "MPS-987654"
    },
    "parcels": [
      {
        "customerReferences": ["000000123", "", "", "12"]
      }
    ]
  },
  "error": []
}
```

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `jobid` | DPD job identifier — used to look up the `dpdconnect_shipping_batch_job` record |
| `shipment.orderId` | Magento order increment ID |
| `shipment.product.productCode` | `RETURN` for return labels, otherwise product code |
| `shipment.trackingInfo.parcelNumbers[0]` | DPD parcel number |
| `shipment.trackingInfo.shipmentIdentifier` | DPD shipment MPS identifier |
| `shipment.parcels[0].customerReferences[3]` | Magento shipment entity ID (used to find correct shipment) |
| `error` | Array — empty on success, contains error objects on failure |

### Processing Logic

```
1. Parse body from php://input
2. Find order by increment_id
3. Find batch job by jobId → find batch
4. Find shipment by customerReferences[3] (entity_id), fallback to first shipment
5a. [errors array non-empty] → set batch + job status = "failed" → return
5b. [success]
    ├── Fetch label PDF: $client->getParcel()->getLabel($parcelNumber)
    ├── Save label to dpdconnect_shipping_label
    ├── Add tracking number to Magento shipment
    └── Set batch + job status = "success"
```

### Response

The endpoint returns no explicit response body. DPD does not use the response.

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED:END -->
